name: Grilled Shark Teriyaki
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse shark with cold water; pat dry with paper towels.
2. Set aside.
3. Drain pineapple and reserve 3 tablespoons of juice; set pineapple chunks aside.
4. Make the marinade by combining reserved juice, soy sauce, sherry, ginger, mustard, garlic, and brown sugar in a bowl. Stir well.
5. Pour the marinade over shark and cover. Refrigerate for 1 hour, turning once.
6. Using bamboo or metal skewers, make kabobs by alternating pineapple and green pepper pieces; set aside.
7. Remove shark from marinade and place on a well-greased grill grate, about 4-5 inches from hot coals.
8. Cook shark 4-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until it flakes with a fork.
9. Baste the shark with marinade and turn. Cook an additional 4-5 minutes, or until just browned.
10. Place the fruit and vegetable kabobs on the grill. Cook 15-30 seconds on each side, or until just browned.
11. Makes 4 servings.
NOTE: Shark may be broiled in a well-greased broiler pan, 5-6 inches from the source of heat. Follow grilling times and directions.
